items.prompt: solves bounded systems of linear equations and interprets bounded linear inequalities in graph, table, and symbolic forms
items.expected_evidence: solve or verify bounded systems of linear equations in multiple representations
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner finds or verifies solutions using methods that preserve the simultaneous-constraint meaning.

items.prompt: explains why a solution pair must satisfy both equations or inequalities and what different system outcomes mean
items.expected_evidence: explain why a solution pair must satisfy both equations or inequalities simultaneously
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains solution meaning as overlap of constraints, not just a computed pair.

items.prompt: Diagnose a peer error aligned to elimination_substitution_drift.
items.expected_evidence: classify bounded system outcomes as one solution, no solution, or infinitely many solutions
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies how the peer broke equivalence or lost a system case distinction.

items.prompt: represents and interprets bounded linear inequalities and simple systems of inequalities in less-familiar coordinate or contextual settings
items.expected_evidence: interpret and graph bounded linear inequalities and overlap regions in simple cases
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves inequality direction and overlap meaning in a varied setting.
